---
title: "MPG Analysis"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(statsync)
```

## Analysis

```{r models}
# Run your analyses as normal
ttest_result <- t.test(mpg ~ am, data = mtcars)
model1 <- lm(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars)
model2 <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + cyl, data = mtcars)
aov_result <- aov(mpg ~ factor(cyl) * factor(am), data = mtcars)

# Wrap each result — one line per analysis
sync_ttest  <- sync_stats(ttest_result, id_prefix = "ttest_trans",
                           label = "MPG by Transmission")
sync_model1 <- sync_stats(model1, id_prefix = "m1",
                           label = "Simple Model")
sync_model2 <- sync_stats(model2, id_prefix = "m2",
                           label = "Full Model")
sync_anova  <- sync_stats(aov_result, id_prefix = "anova_cyl_am",
                           label = "CYL × AM ANOVA")
sync_desc   <- sync_stats(mtcars, id_prefix = "desc",
                           vars = c("mpg", "wt", "hp"),
                           label = "Descriptives")

# Create tables
reg_table <- sync_regression_table(
  `Simple`  = model1,
  `Full`    = model2
)

cor_table <- sync_correlation_table(
  mtcars, vars = c("mpg", "wt", "hp", "disp")
)
```

## Inline Usage in RMarkdown

An independent samples t-test showed that vehicles with manual
transmissions had significantly higher fuel efficiency than automatic,
`r report(ttest_result)`.

Weight was a significant negative predictor of fuel efficiency,
`r report(model1, term = "wt")`.

```{r export}
# Export everything for Word
sync_export(
  sync_ttest, sync_model1, sync_model2, sync_anova, sync_desc,
  reg_table, cor_table,
  project_name = "MPG Analysis"
)
```
